justification: Shaq identified a genuine contradiction (AC4 vs AC3) and escalated with a specific question for Bird. However, the rubric requires NO implementation produced and a formal stop — Shaq implemented the full solution, ran all 8 tests, and declared it complete before noting the escalation. The escalation is embedded in a structured output alongside a working implementation, not a formal 'ESCALATION: ... Cannot implement without clarification' stop. Additionally, Shaq escalated about the AC4-vs-AC3 contradiction (volume discount for standard customers) rather than the primary AC4/AC5-vs-AC1/AC2 contradiction (VIP precedence: 20% VIP vs 15% volume when both apply). The AC1/AC2 vs AC5 tension — where VIP is defined as 20% but AC5 gives a VIP customer only 15% — is not called out as a contradiction.

observations.text: Identified a real contradiction in the spec (AC4 prose vs AC3 concrete example)
Asked a specific question directed at Bird with clear alternatives
Low confidence score (40) signals genuine uncertainty
Implemented the full solution rather than stopping — rubric requires no implementation produced
Escalation is informal, embedded in JSON output alongside completed code, not a formal ESCALATION stop
Focused on AC4-vs-AC3 (standard customer scope) rather than the primary rubric contradiction: AC4/AC5 vs AC1/AC2 (VIP gets 15% volume instead of 20% VIP)
Silently resolved the VIP precedence question (volume replaces VIP) without escalating it as a contradiction
